name: Process Metadata
description: Contextual data about a running process, which may include information such as environment variables, image name, user/owner, command line arguments, and parent-child relationships. Unlike process creation events (which capture the moment of execution), process metadata often refers to the static or snapshot attributes of a process currently resident in memory.

commands.description: Lists running processes with metadata like memory usage, user context, and window title.

commands.command: Get-Process | Select-Object Name, Id, Path, StartTime, MainWindowTitle
commands.description: Retrieves rich object-based metadata for running processes.

commands.command: wmic process get Name, ProcessId, CommandLine, ParentProcessId
commands.description: Queries WMI for specific process attributes.

sysinternals.description: Advanced inspection of process handles, loaded DLLs, and security tokens.

filesystem.description: The proc filesystem provides a direct interface to kernel data structures for processes.

commands.description: Displays detailed process status, including owner, CPU/Mem usage, and full command line.
Lists open files and network connections associated with a process.

cross_platform_query.query: SELECT pid, name, path, cmdline, uid, parent, on_disk FROM processes;
cross_platform_query.description: SQL-based query to retrieve structured metadata across Windows, Linux, and macOS.
